key_points: Food resources include crops and animals that provide essential nutrients for humans
Improvement in food resources aims to increase production and ensure food security
Crop variety improvement focuses on high yield, disease resistance and better quality
Biotic factors such as pests and diseases and abiotic factors such as drought affect crop production
Manure improves soil structure and fertility while fertilizers provide specific nutrients to plants
Macronutrients like nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium are required in large amounts for plant growth
Irrigation methods include wells, canals, river lift systems and modern irrigation systems
Cropping patterns such as mixed cropping, intercropping and crop rotation help maintain soil fertility
Crop protection includes controlling weeds, pests and diseases using different methods
Animal husbandry involves breeding, feeding and health care of livestock
Poultry farming produces eggs and meat while cattle farming provides milk and farm labour
Fish production includes capture fishing and culture fishing such as aquaculture
examples: Mixed cropping example: Wheat and gram
Fish farming example: Composite fish culture
formulas: Hybridisation → Crossing genetically different plants to obtain improved crop varieties
Intercropping → Growing two or more crops in definite rows on the same field
Crop rotation → Growing different crops sequentially to maintain soil fertility
Aquaculture → Farming of aquatic organisms such as fish and prawns
Apiculture → Scientific management of honey bees for honey production
exam_tips: Understand objectives of crop variety improvement
Remember differences between manure and fertilizers
Study advantages of intercropping and crop rotation
Learn different irrigation methods used in agriculture
Understand the role of livestock in food production
Remember definitions of aquaculture and apiculture
